_description: Non-table-backed controlled vocabularies used across the extraction schema

ipcc_action_type._source: IPCC AR6 Chapter 14 adaptation action typology

values.name: Structural/Physical
values.description: Engineered and built environment, technological, ecosystem-based adaptation

values.name: Social
values.description: Educational, informational, behavioral

values.name: Institutional
values.description: Economic, laws/regulations, government policies and programs

values.name: Ecosystem-based
values.description: Nature-based solutions, green infrastructure, ecological restoration

implementing_actor_type._source: Derived from case library and CDP data analysis
implementing_actor_type._note: Use multiple actor types in arrays instead of 'multi_stakeholder' to preserve specific actor information

values.name: Anecdotal
values.description: Qualitative reports without systematic measurement

values.name: Measured
values.description: Quantitative data collected but not rigorously controlled

values.name: Rigorously Evaluated
values.description: Controlled studies, peer-reviewed evaluations

values.name: Case Library
values.description: Curated research from the existing 200+ case library

values.name: CDP
values.description: City self-reported data from CDP disclosure

values.name: Academic
values.description: Peer-reviewed journal articles, conference papers

values.name: Government
values.description: Government reports, plans, policy documents

values.name: News
values.description: News articles, press releases

values.name: NGO
values.description: Reports from NGOs, international organizations

values.name: Measured
values.description: Quantitative data from direct measurement

values.name: Modeled
values.description: Output from simulation or modeling

values.name: Anecdotal
values.description: Qualitative or informal observation

values.name: Self-reported
values.description: CDP and similar self-disclosure. Default for CDP data.

values.name: Expert Assessment
values.description: Professional judgment without systematic data

mechanism_seed_vocabulary._description: Seed vocabulary for mechanisms dimension. Free text — these are suggestions, not constraints. Expanded in Sprint 4 to include tech-native mechanism types alongside original eight. Will be further formalized through corpus clustering in Month 2.
mechanism_seed_vocabulary._note: Sprint 4: added sense_and_detect, forecast_and_model, automate_and_control, inform_and_alert. 'monitor' is retained for general observation but now complemented by these more specific digital-process types. The distinction: 'monitor' = ongoing observation (often human-led or passive); 'sense_and_detect' = active digital sensing with discrete event detection; 'automate_and_control' = closed-loop machine actuation. See decisions-log.md §15.

values.name: Absorb
values.description: Buffer, attenuate, or absorb the impact (e.g., green infrastructure absorbing stormwater)

values.name: Redirect
values.description: Divert, channel, or redistribute the hazard (e.g., flood channels)

values.name: Harden
values.description: Strengthen, fortify, or protect against impact (e.g., building hardening)

values.name: Monitor
values.description: Observe, measure, or track conditions over time without automated response (e.g., manual sensor readings, periodic surveys, environmental monitoring programs)

values.name: Govern
values.description: Regulate, plan, coordinate, or incentivize (e.g., building codes, zoning)

values.name: Shift Risk
values.description: Transfer, insure, or redistribute risk (e.g., parametric insurance)

values.name: Adapt Behavior
values.description: Change practices, educate, or build awareness (e.g., demand response)

values.name: Restore/Regenerate
values.description: Rehabilitate, restore, or regenerate natural or built systems (e.g., ecosystem restoration)

values.name: Sense and Detect
values.description: Actively sense environmental or system conditions and detect discrete events or threshold crossings using digital sensors, IoT devices, or remote sensing (e.g., flood sensors triggering alerts, wildfire detection cameras, structural health monitoring)

values.name: Forecast and Model
values.description: Use computational models, AI/ML, or simulation to produce forward-looking predictions or scenario analyses (e.g., hydrological models forecasting flood depth, digital twins simulating heat distribution, ML-based drought prediction)

values.name: Automate and Control
values.description: Execute closed-loop machine actuation or automated system control in response to sensed conditions without requiring human decision in the loop (e.g., automated pump activation during storm events, DERMS load balancing, smart irrigation responding to soil moisture sensors)

values.name: Inform and Alert
values.description: Disseminate information, warnings, or targeted communications to populations, responders, or decision-makers to trigger human action (e.g., heat-wave SMS alerts, early-warning broadcasts, real-time dashboard for emergency managers). Distinct from automate_and_control — the response is human.

values.name: Grants
values.description: Grant funding from government, foundations, or international organizations

values.name: Municipal Budget
values.description: City's own budget and resources

values.name: National Government Funding
values.description: National-level public finance

values.name: Regional Funds and Programmes
values.description: Regional development banks, EU funds, etc.

values.name: Public-Private Partnership (PPP)
values.description: Structured partnership between public and private sectors

values.name: Commercial Bank Loans
values.description: Loans from commercial banking sector

values.name: Development Bank Financing
values.description: World Bank, regional development banks, etc.

values.name: Climate Funds
values.description: Green Climate Fund, Adaptation Fund, etc.

values.name: Green Bonds
values.description: Municipal or corporate green bonds

values.name: Blended Finance
values.description: Mix of public, private, and philanthropic capital

values.name: Private Sector Investment
values.description: Direct private investment

values.name: International ODA
values.description: Official development assistance

values.name: Fully Funded
values.description: Complete funding secured

values.name: Partially Funded
values.description: Some funding secured, seeking additional

values.name: Seeking Full Funding
values.description: No funding secured, seeking complete funding

values.name: Seeking Partial Funding
values.description: No funding secured, seeking partial funding

values.name: Feasibility Stage
values.description: Feasibility studies and finance-ready project preparation in progress

values.name: Not Applicable
values.description: Financing not required or not relevant

equity_focus._source: EDF equity framework (Shi 2024 rubric) — adapted for solution-level classification

values.name: None
values.description: No equity framing documented in the solution

values.name: Co-Benefit
values.description: Equity benefits are secondary — not the design target, but documented as a co-benefit for vulnerable populations

values.name: Primary Target
values.description: Solution is explicitly designed to serve, protect, or empower vulnerable populations

justice_dimension._source: Shi (2024) equity rubric — four justice dimensions for climate adaptation outcomes
justice_dimension._note: Applied to Outcome nodes to characterize what type of equity impact is documented. Multiple dimensions can apply to a single outcome. See decisions-log.md §16.

values.name: Distributive
values.description: Who bears the costs and who receives the benefits — distributional fairness of outcomes and burdens

values.name: Procedural
values.description: Who participates in and shapes decisions — fairness of decision-making processes

values.name: Recognitional
values.description: Whose knowledge, history, and lived experience are acknowledged and meaningfully incorporated

values.name: Epistemic
values.description: How knowledge is produced, what counts as valid evidence, and whose expertise is legitimized
Policy mandates or requires the action
Policy or mechanism enables or supports the action
Policy issued by an actor
Actor implements the action
Policy or condition blocks or constrains the action
Action constrained by regulation or resource limitation
